subject: Statistics
title: Confidence Levels Explained
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Confidence Levels Explained.

hook.text: '95% confidence' sounds rock-solid. But it means 1 in 20 polls is expected to be flat-out wrong.

buildup.text: A confidence level is the probability that the interval captures the true value if repeated many times.

discovery.text: 95% is the convention, not a law. Some fields use 99%. Particle physics requires 99.99997% — '5 sigma.'

twist.text: Higher confidence means wider intervals. 99% certainty requires admitting you know less precisely.

climax.text: Confidence levels are a trade-off: more certainty means less precision. You can't have both for free.

punchline.text: More confidence, wider range. Certainty has a price.

quiz.question: What does '95% confidence' actually mean?
options: If you repeated the poll many times, 95% of intervals would contain the true value
There's a 95% chance the sample is correct
95% of respondents gave the same answer
